design_goal: Enable a security admin to configure, launch, and review an SMS phishing simulation campaign using the existing campaign wizard, while handling SMS-specific constraints (phone coverage, character limits, legal disclosure, mobile debrief) with clarity and without requiring new mental models.
user_segments: IT admin configuring employee phone data
constraints: No existing phone number data model in Dune — net-new infrastructure
TCPA compliance required — legal clearance not yet confirmed
Standard SMS limited to 160 characters (GSM-7) or 70 characters (Unicode)
v1 personalization scope unconfirmed (AI vs. template-based)
Risk scoring integration for SMS click signal not yet designed
Sender identity strategy (short code, 10DLC, toll-free) not decided
selected_strategy: Channel-extended existing wizard (Option A) with multi-channel-capable data model (Option C architecture)
selected_strategy_rationale: Extends the existing email phishing wizard with a channel type selector at step 1. SMS-specific screens replace email-specific screens where needed. All existing patterns — group targeting, scheduling, exclusion windows, review-before-launch — carry over unchanged. The underlying campaign data model is designed for multi-channel from the start, enabling coordinated email + SMS campaigns as a future upgrade without retrofitting.

alternative_strategies.name: Dedicated SMS campaign section
alternative_strategies.summary: A new SMS Simulation entry point in the navigation with a wizard optimized from scratch for SMS.
alternative_strategies.rejected_because: Two parallel wizards to maintain; campaign management splits by channel when it should be unified by goal; duplicates significant logic; Proofpoint took this approach and it reduced adoption.

alternative_strategies.name: Channel-first multi-channel wizard
alternative_strategies.summary: Redesign the campaign wizard to support multiple channels (email + SMS) simultaneously from step 1.
alternative_strategies.rejected_because: Requires both email and SMS delivery to be production-ready simultaneously; significantly higher v1 complexity; harder to scope or cut if SMS delivery slips. Correct long-term direction but wrong v1 scope.

tradeoffs: Coordinated multi-channel attack simulation (a meaningful differentiator) cannot ship in v1 — admins must create two separate campaigns and manually coordinate timing
If TCPA legal clearance changes the requirement from acknowledgment to explicit pre-campaign consent, the review step design will need to be rebuilt
If v1 ships template-based only, the personalization story must be carefully calibrated — Smart Templates framing, not AI framing
assumptions: The existing email phishing campaign wizard is the correct structural reference
Phone number data will be importable via CSV in v1 at minimum
The TCPA acknowledgment checkbox at the review step is a minimum viable disclosure mechanism
Delivery spread (randomized send window) is technically feasible in v1
Bot-click filtering is handled at the infrastructure layer (Eng confirms)
open_issues: TCPA legal clearance: if explicit employee consent is required (not just admin acknowledgment), a new pre-campaign employee notice flow is needed — changes Step 6 significantly
AI vs. template-based v1: if AI personalization is confirmed for v1, Step 3 (Message) needs a personalization preview mode — significant screen-level change
Sender identity: short code vs. 10DLC changes the SMS preview sender display in Step 3 and potentially the debrief copy
STOP reply permanence: if permanent across all campaigns, the coverage indicator needs an Opted Out segment separate from Missing
Risk score signal weighting: if SMS clicks are a new signal type (not equivalent to email), the campaign detail risk score delta column needs channel-aware labeling
risks: TCPA blocker: if legal clearance arrives late, the review step needs redesign
Phone coverage cold-start: feature is immediately limited by phone number data quality; onboarding path for phone data must be visible before first campaign is created
AI personalization gap: if v1 ships template-based, marketing must use Smart Templates framing — not AI framing
Bot-click filtering transparency: if Eng cannot surface filtered counts, the campaign results will be trusted less by sophisticated admins
new_patterns_required: SMS bubble preview panel in message editor (Step 3) — renders message as a native SMS chat bubble at 360px; requires DS review for standardization across SMS and future vishing contexts
Phone coverage progress indicator (Step 2 and Group detail) — coverage bar + count inline with group selector; requires DS review if not already a standard group metadata pattern
Mobile debrief landing page — entirely new learner-facing surface at 375px; outside the admin UI design system; requires its own component library or a documented extension of Stillsuit DS v2
next_actions: Resolve TCPA question with PM before starting Figma — acknowledgment checkbox vs. pre-campaign employee notice flow are very different screen states
Get Eng confirmation on v1 personalization scope (AI vs. template) — determines whether Step 3 is a template library or a personalization wizard
Open Group detail view in Figma and map the phone coverage tab against existing Group component — most contained new surface, good starting point
Design the mobile debrief landing page first — highest-stakes new surface, most clearly scoped, no unresolved decisions block it
Confirm with Eng which sender identity will be used in v1 so SMS preview panel renders the correct sender format
